description_long: This object is a historical-style field cannon mounted on a wooden carriage. The cannon barrel is long, cylindrical, and appears to be made of dark green or black metal, featuring decorative rings and a flared muzzle. The carriage is constructed from wood, with four solid wooden wheels attached by metal axles and brackets, and reinforced with metal fittings. The cannon is secured to the carriage with visible metal fastenings and has side handles for maneuvering. The overall design is robust, with the carriage providing stability and mobility for the heavy barrel. There are no visible modern features such as armor plating, camouflage, or advanced weapon mounts.
description: A historical field cannon with a long metal barrel mounted on a sturdy wooden carriage with four wheels and metal fittings. The design is robust and functional, with visible fastenings and handles.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full length of the cannon barrel and the symmetrical arrangement of the four wooden wheels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long metal barrel, wooden carriage, and the attachment of the wheels and metal fittings.
description_view_2: Front view focusing on the cannon's muzzle and the width of the carriage, with the wheels and barrel alignment clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the length of the barrel and carriage details from the other side.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the wooden carriage; no additional weapon mounts visible.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; surfaces are solid dark green and brown.
